prerequisites: An accessible Akeneo PIM instance and its host URL (for example https://your-instance.cloud.akeneo.com), which you provide in the host config field.
Permission in the PIM UI to create a connection under Connect > Connection settings, which generates the credentials below.
A connection's Client ID and Secret (generated in the PIM UI) for the client_id and secret config fields.
The connection's auto-generated API username and password (shown once in the connection's Credentials section) for the api_username and password config fields.

authMethods.name: OAuth password grant (client credentials + API user)
authMethods.summary: Akeneo's REST API uses an OAuth 2.0 password grant. A POST to /api/oauth/v1/token carries an Authorization: Basic header containing the Base64-encoded client_id:secret, plus grant_type=password with the API username and password in the body. The returned access_token is then sent as a Bearer token on all API requests. This connector collects client_id, secret (the secret field), api_username, and password to perform that flow.

setupSteps.title: Create a connection in the PIM
setupSteps.body: Log into your Akeneo PIM, go to Connect > Connection settings, click Create, give the connection a label, select a flow type, and save. This generates the credentials the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Collect the connection credentials
setupSteps.body: From the connection's Credentials section, copy the Client ID and Secret. The API username and password are auto-generated and displayed there as well; use Regenerate if you need to view them again.

setupSteps.title: Provide the host
setupSteps.body: Set host to the base URL of your PIM instance (for example https://your-instance.cloud.akeneo.com). All API calls are made directly against this instance domain (under /api/rest/v1).

setupSteps.title: Enter the credentials in the connector
setupSteps.body: Set client_id to the connection's Client ID, secret to the connection's Secret, api_username to the API username, and password to the API password. The connector uses these to request an OAuth access token and authenticate REST API calls.
